response:
Nous sommes capables... de déployer une puissance de feu... supérieure à tout ce qui a jamais été déchaîné dans l’histoire de la guerre.

This translation preserves the original's dramatic, halting cadence via the placed ellipses, uses natural French military-adjacent vocabulary to match the ominous, weighty tone of the original line, and retains its full core meaning. "Déployer" (to deploy/bRing to bear) fits the context of massing firepower better than a literal translation of "launch", while "déchaîné" (unleashed) captures the gravity of "released" in a wartime context far more naturally than a literal translation.
